scope: Pass #3 — targeted gap hunt after 4 prior passes. Italian tier-2 cities (Naples, Bologna, Turin — all new 2024-2025 taxes), additional German cities (Dresden, Leipzig), Swiss resorts (Lucerne), Croatian coast (Split), Saudi Makkah/Madinah, US states (Colorado, Arizona, Pennsylvania, Massachusetts).
methodology: 12 WebSearches across three tracks (new 2020-2024 changes, specialty rule patterns, US state gaps). Pre-filtered to rules that use canonical BookingContext fields and values — i.e., will fire in the current engine.
summary: Diminishing returns confirmed but still found 7 new rate changes and 10 new booking-affecting rules. Highlights: Italian Jubilee-year wave expanded to tier-2 cities (Naples/Bologna/Turin started 2024-2025); 4 more US states with permanent-resident 30-day exemptions; Dresden/Vienna/Frankfurt specialty student-hostel exemptions.

findings.summary: Naples introduced higher tourist tax rates from March 1, 2025. €3-5.50/person/night hotels (tiered), €5 STR flat. 14-night cap.

rates.source_quote: From March 1, 2025, Naples introduced higher tourist tax rates. Short-term rentals pay €5 per person, while other hotels pay between €3 and €5.50.
rates.notes: Using STR €5 flat as the seeded value; per-property-type tiers would need tier structure.

rules.name: 14-night cap
rules.description: Tax applied only on first 14 consecutive nights of a booking
rules.source_quote: The tax is only applied on the first 14 consecutive nights of a booking.

findings.summary: Bologna tourist tax started July 1, 2024. 10.5% of room rate, capped at €7/person/night, 5 consecutive nights. Under 14 exempt.

rates.source_quote: Collection began on July 1, 2024, with rates of 10.5% with a maximum limit of 7 euros per person per night of stay and 5 consecutive nights.

rules.name: €7 per-night cap
rules.description: Tax capped at €7 per person per night even if 10.5% of room rate would be higher

rules.name: Under 14 exempt
rules.source_quote: does not apply to children under 14 for a maximum consecutive stay of 5 days.

findings.summary: Turin tourist tax started September 1, 2024. €2.30-5/person/night by star rating.

rates.source_quote: Collection started on September 1, 2024, with rates ranging from €2.30 to €5.00 per person per night for boutique hotels depending on quality rating.
rates.notes: Using upper bound €5 as the seeded flat value; proper star-tiered modeling would need a tiered rate.

findings.summary: Dresden 6% accommodation tax. Under-27 guests on educational stays exempt.

rates.source_quote: Dresden has a 6% accommodation tax (from 18 years old, except for under 27-year-olds staying overnight for educational purposes).

rules.name: Student (educational stay) exempt
rules.description: Dresden exempts guests under 27 on educational/university stays

findings.summary: Vienna youth hostels exempt from the 3.2% Ortstaxe.

rules.name: Youth hostels exempt
rules.description: Properties classified as youth hostels are exempt from the Vienna Ortstaxe
rules.source_quote: Vienna: Youth hostels are exempt from the 3.2% local tax.

findings.summary: Frankfurt €2 tourist tax (since 2018); students and teachers exempt with school certificate.

rules.name: Student exempt with school certificate
rules.source_quote: Frankfurt: Students and teachers are exempt from the €2 tourist tax when presenting a school certificate.

findings.summary: Makkah 5% municipal tax on 4-5 star hotels, 2.5% on other pilgrim accommodations, effective February 14, 2025.

rates.source_quote: A 5% tax on 4 and 5 star hotels and a 2.5% on all other accommodation housing pilgrims was imposed from February 14th by the municipalities of Makkah and Madinah.
rates.notes: Star-tiered; seeded value is upper bound. Proper modeling needs star_rating condition.

findings.summary: Madinah 5% municipal tax on 4-5 star hotels, 2.5% on others, from Feb 14, 2025.

rules.name: Permanent resident (30+ days written agreement)
rules.description: Colorado sales tax exempt on accommodation rented to permanent resident with written agreement for ≥30 consecutive days
rules.source_quote: The rental of rooms and accommodations to a permanent resident for a period of at least 30 consecutive days is exempt from sales tax.

rules.name: Long-term residential rental (30+ days)
rules.description: Arizona classifies rentals ≥30 days as residential rental, exempt from transient privilege tax
rules.source_quote: Residential rental is the rental of real property for a period of more than 29 days for residential purposes.

rules.name: Permanent resident (30+ days)
rules.description: Pennsylvania: stays ≥ 30 consecutive days make the guest a permanent resident, exempt from hotel occupancy tax
rules.source_quote: In Pennsylvania, to be a permanent resident exempt from the tax, a person must occupy the property, or have the right to occupy the property, for 30 consecutive days or more.

rules.name: Long-stay (90+ days)
rules.description: Massachusetts room occupancy excise applies only to rentals of 90 days or less
rules.source_quote: The room occupancy excise tax applies to room rentals of 90 days or less in hotels, motels, bed and breakfast establishments, and lodging houses.

findings.summary: Split tourist tax €2/person/night (Category A coastal municipality).

deferred_for_taxonomy_extension.needs: Jurisdiction code DE-SN-LEJ doesn't exist in DB; skipped

summary_of_this_pass.recommendation: After this pass, the dominant remaining gap is TAXONOMY (nonprofit_*, military, disability, school_group values for guest_type) rather than research. Recommend extending BookingContext canonical values before another breadth pass — that would unlock the 9 rules filtered from breadth-v2 plus 4 rules deferred from this pass, for total of 13+ rules without any additional research.
